parameters.jsCode:
// Input text from transcription
const text = $json.content?.parts?.[0]?.text || "";

// Generic and strong prompt for email extraction
const prompt = `
You are an expert email extractor and validator.

Your task:
- Find any email address mentioned in the text.
- Handle cases where the email may be split or obfuscated, e.g.:
  - extra spaces ("name @ gmail . com"),
  - words instead of symbols ("name at gmail dot com"),
  - local-part broken into multiple words/numbers before "@"
    (e.g., "john doe 123 @gmail.com" → "johndoe123@gmail.com").
- Normalize by:
  - converting to lowercase,
  - removing unnecessary spaces,
  - fixing common domain typos (e.g., "gamil.com" → "gmail.com",
    "yaho.com" → "yahoo.com", "hotmai.com" → "hotmail.com",
    "outlok.com" → "outlook.com"),
  - stripping trailing punctuation.
- Do not invent an address that is not in the text.
- If no email exists, return null.
- Output ONLY valid JSON with one key "recipient".

Text: """${text}"""
`;

return [{ json: { prompt } }];
